speaker: Old Woman
description: Voice-reference dialogue samples for the recurring Old Woman NPC in Pig & Swine RPG. These are draft samples, not final committed game text.
source_role_summary: Old Town and Office Street flavour character
Street-level witness to missing filings and printer rumours in Chapter 3
Human-scale impact / protest NPC in Chapter 5

voice_profile.core_voice: elderly, observant, nosy, warm underneath, sharp-tongued, practical, neighbourhood-wise, mildly prophetic in the way old neighbours can be
comedy_engine: says legally insightful things as gossip
treats pigeons as a disciplined but corrupt legal faction
has seen every firm, landlord, clerk, and reform plan become ridiculous eventually
delivers warnings through domestic details: windows, crumbs, notices, queues, coats, benches
is funny because she is not impressed by anyone's institutional self-importance
do: make her sharp but kind underneath
make her a street-level witness to the city changing
let her give small hints without sounding like a tutorial
use pigeons as a running motif
give her social memory of the Legal Quarter
do_not: make her speak in broken English
turn every line into a pigeon joke

relationship_dynamics.Dr. A. Cula: calls him young lawyer or doctor-lawyer; gradually respects that he listens
relationship_dynamics.Asia: respects her as the only person who knows where things are
relationship_dynamics.Mr. Pig: thinks he is loud but harmless; compares him to a wet harbour bell
relationship_dynamics.Mr. Murrow: respects his calm; says he has funeral-director posture around bad files
relationship_dynamics.Crab: likes his suspicion because it resembles proper stairwell vigilance
relationship_dynamics.Whimsy: finds him theatrical but useful at frightening pigeons and bureaucrats
relationship_dynamics.Waldek: sympathetic; sees his home case as exactly the sort of quiet cruelty people miss
relationship_dynamics.Kowalski: knows him as another watcher, though too proud of his notebooks
relationship_dynamics.Bajtek: believes him about machines more than she believes most lawyers
relationship_dynamics.Advocate Szpon: distrusts his polished shoes and deadline smile
relationship_dynamics.Supreme Court Judge: does not know personally, but later views the judgment as proof someone listened

notes: Every line is a voice-reference draft for later rewriting into godot/data/dialogues/dialogues.json.

speaker: Old Woman
text: The office used to be quieter before the printer learned procedure.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Feed one pigeon and it brings friends. File one vague notice and it brings lawyers.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Young lawyer, if paper looks too clean, someone is hiding the dirt elsewhere.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: I have lived here long enough to know that every reform begins by moving the benches.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: A deadline is just a clock with an opinion.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: The pigeons understand hierarchy. They also understand crumbs, which makes them more honest than some committees.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: When officials say everyone was informed, ask who understood.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: A building can look tidy while treating people badly. I have known landlords like that.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: If you want to know what is happening in a district, ask the windows before the offices.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Your firm is ridiculous. That is not the same as useless.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: People who polish their shoes too much often expect others to slip.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: I do not trust notices that use long words for short threats.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: A queue is where the city hides responsibility and calls it order.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: The law is like bread. If it never reaches the table, speeches about bakeries do not help.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Do not underestimate old women on benches. We are the archive with better weather.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Careful. That pigeon has the eyes of a debt collector.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: No, you may not feed them court documents. They develop opinions.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: The grey one is called Professor. He steals crumbs and blames procedure.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Sit if you like. The bench has heard worse cases than yours.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: I feed pigeons because they are direct about wanting things.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: That one limps. Still faster than City Hall.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Do not stand there looking important. You will attract officials.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: I have crumbs, gossip, and limited patience.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: The pigeons know who walks past before bad news arrives.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Young lawyer, your shoes say first week. Your face says first file.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: If you are lost, start by asking why everyone else looks certain.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: This street has watched three reforms, two scandals, and one bakery become a consultancy.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: The pigeons disliked Szpon before the lawyers did.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: If the court is north, trouble is usually already halfway there.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: I keep crumbs in one pocket and old notices in the other. Both attract pests.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: If people are whispering about a case, follow the person carrying too many papers.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Asia knows where things are. Ask her before asking a man who says strategic.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Mr. Murrow looks like a closed door, but he usually opens onto the right corridor.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Crab will notice what others stepped over.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Whimsy will make a speech. Listen for the one useful sentence inside it.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: If a notice frightens everyone, read the small line that tells them what to do next.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: If a landlord says harmony, ask whose window became too loud.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: If the printer is blamed too quickly, look for the person who benefits from paper going missing.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: If a big plan says public interest, ask which public was allowed to answer.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: If something is urgent, first find the person who knew yesterday.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Do not chase every rumour. Chase the one with a date.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: A witness who complains about everything is useful when they did not complain.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: A file with no dates is a basket with no bottom.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: If people say the system worked, ask who came out of it unheard.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Keep your stamped copy. Memory is for soup recipes, not litigation.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Residential District is trouble today. You can smell wet notices from here.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Business District? Hm. People there smile like doors closing.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: City Hall is open, but so is a mousetrap.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Airport Road? Running away is for men who have already packed badly.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: The Supreme Court stairs are not for beginners. They make even pigeons walk respectfully.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: That road is blocked by construction and confidence. Both are dangerous.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: No reason to go there yet, young lawyer. The plot has not unlocked that anxiety.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Come back when someone has served the wrong paper to the right person.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: That way leads to glass offices. Take a better argument with you.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: The city lets you see future trouble so you can worry efficiently.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: First day at Pig & Swine?
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Do not answer. Your suit is still optimistic.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: The office used to be quieter before the printer learned procedure.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: If Mr. Pig is shouting, the firm is either bankrupt or awake.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Asia is inside. She knows where everything is and where everyone should have been ten minutes ago.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Mr. Murrow is the calm one. That means he has already imagined the disaster and filed it alphabetically.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Crab looks rude because he is measuring the room for mistakes.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Whimsy is probably west at the café, frightening pastries with speeches.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: You should go in before the pigeons assign you a seniority number.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Welcome to the street, young lawyer. Try not to step in precedent.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Go inside. The loud one must be Mr. Pig unless the kettle learned finance.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: If the founder is pacing, it means money has entered the conversation.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Do not wait for courage. The court never does.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: The door sticks on rainy days and financial crises.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Asia will point you to the shouting.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Do not be frightened by the sign. It has survived worse spelling.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Pig & Swine is open, which surprises everyone, including the sign.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: The pigeons are taking bets on how long before you come back holding a binder.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Go on. First days do not age well outside.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: If you hear paper falling, that is probably normal.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
context: after player has met Mr. Pig
text: So you met Mr. Pig. You still have both ears. Good.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
context: after player has met Mr. Pig
text: He shouts about drowning even when standing on cobblestones.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
context: after player has met Mr. Pig
text: Financial panic is loud, but the legal problem is usually quieter.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
context: after player has met Mr. Pig
text: Find the calm person. That will be Mr. Murrow.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
context: after player has met Mr. Pig
text: When a man speaks of crisis before breakfast, check the deadline.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
context: after player has met Mr. Pig
text: Do not let the maritime language distract you. Ships do not get served court papers.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
context: after player has met Mr. Pig
text: Asia will know which pile is today's catastrophe.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
context: after player has met Mr. Pig
text: Mr. Pig means well. He just means it at full volume.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
context: after player has met Mr. Pig
text: The pigeons have downgraded the firm from sinking to damp.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
context: after player has met Mr. Pig
text: Go find the file before the file finds you.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: The heavy book? I saw a young man carrying it like a moral injury.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: If a binder frightens the floorboards, it is probably legal.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Try the archive shelves. Important things hide where dust becomes policy.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Asia may know the shelf. Asia knows shelves like generals know maps.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Crab looked at that binder yesterday with professional disgust.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Do not lift it with your back. Procedure is heavier than justice.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: If the case is about service, the rule is probably in the thick book no one wants to open.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: A binder that heavy either contains law or someone's lunch for winter.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Find it before the court asks you what rule you mean.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: The pigeons refuse to sit on it. That tells you something.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Crab notices mistakes because he expects them.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: If the address is wrong, Crab will look pleased in a terrible way.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: He is not unfriendly. He is factually disappointed.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Ask him about service. He likes envelopes less than I do.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Crab can tell the difference between delivery and theatre.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Do not ask if he is happy. Ask if the notice makes sense.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: He has the face of a man who distrusts stamps.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: If he says impossible, write that down.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: The pigeons respect him. He never drops crumbs by accident.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Go ask Crab before the court asks you.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
context: hint toward Whimsy at Café Paragraf
text: The café is west. Follow the smell of coffee and unnecessary principles.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
context: hint toward Whimsy at Café Paragraf
text: Whimsy is there when he is not elsewhere being almost useful.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
context: hint toward Whimsy at Café Paragraf
text: He speaks in storms, but sometimes lightning hits the right tree.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
context: hint toward Whimsy at Café Paragraf
text: If you need fairness to sound less like homework, find him.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
context: hint toward Whimsy at Café Paragraf
text: Bring a memo. He respects paper if it is dramatic enough.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
context: hint toward Whimsy at Café Paragraf
text: The café table near the window collects lawyers and crumbs.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
context: hint toward Whimsy at Café Paragraf
text: If you hear someone comparing courts to soup, you have found him.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
context: hint toward Whimsy at Café Paragraf
text: Do not let him draft the whole argument. Let him sharpen one edge.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
context: hint toward Whimsy at Café Paragraf
text: The pigeons avoid his scarf. Too much movement.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
context: hint toward Whimsy at Café Paragraf
text: Go west, young lawyer. The pastry district awaits.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Court is north.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Not metaphorically. North.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Take the file, not the feeling.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: If Mr. Murrow says you are ready, believe him once.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: The clerk will want order. Clerks always do. It is why society still has corners.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Bring the service point first.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Then fairness.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Then ask for the small remedy.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Do not ask the judge to save the firm. Ask the judge to hear the client.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: And drink water. Lawyers forget they are mammals.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: You came back with less panic on you.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: So the client gets heard after all?
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Good. Doors should not close because someone pushed the paper through the wrong slot.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Mr. Pig walked past saying the ship has reached harbour. He was on a pavement.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Asia smiled like someone who found three minutes in a drawer.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Mr. Murrow looked almost approving, which may be illegal.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Crab nodded at a document. That is a parade for him.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Whimsy told the pigeons justice had fluttered. They remained unmoved.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: You did well, young lawyer.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Now keep the next deadline before it becomes a legend.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
context: if player fails/returns after weak court attempt
text: Back already?
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
context: if player fails/returns after weak court attempt
text: You look like the judge asked for the point.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
context: if player fails/returns after weak court attempt
text: That happens when a pile pretends to be an argument.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
context: if player fails/returns after weak court attempt
text: Go inside and sort the file.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
context: if player fails/returns after weak court attempt
text: Service first.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
context: if player fails/returns after weak court attempt
text: Then why it mattered.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
context: if player fails/returns after weak court attempt
text: Then what you want the court to do.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
context: if player fails/returns after weak court attempt
text: No, not everything. Just the proper thing.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
context: if player fails/returns after weak court attempt
text: The pigeons understand sequence. Watch: crumb, fight, victory.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
context: if player fails/returns after weak court attempt
text: Try again before the day grows teeth.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
context: Residential District opens / Old Woman comments
text: Residential District is open now.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
context: Residential District opens / Old Woman comments
text: That means someone has threatened a home.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
context: Residential District opens / Old Woman comments
text: It is always a home before it becomes a file.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
context: Residential District opens / Old Woman comments
text: Waldek lives where the balconies look worried.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
context: Residential District opens / Old Woman comments
text: Do not rush past the windows. They know more than the entrance buzzer.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
context: Residential District opens / Old Woman comments
text: The landlord's office has rules with polished words.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
context: Residential District opens / Old Woman comments
text: The neighbours have the facts with rough edges.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
context: Residential District opens / Old Woman comments
text: Ask both.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
context: Residential District opens / Old Woman comments
text: And if anyone says the poster is the whole problem, ask why eviction followed it.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
context: Residential District opens / Old Woman comments
text: Go south-west, young lawyer.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Tenants' Row hears everything through pipes and letters.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Someone has been taping notices like wallpaper.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: The building rules change tone when management is annoyed.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: A poster in a window is very loud to people who dislike being answered.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Waldek looked smaller after the last letter.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: That is what threats do. They make rooms shrink.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Kowalski watches curtains as if they owe him rent.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Zielinska watches people. More useful.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: The landlord's office smells of polish and pre-decided conclusions.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: If you find a complaint log, check whether the complaints arrived before the anger.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Waldek is frightened, so speak plainly.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: People facing eviction hear every sentence as a door closing.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Ask about the letters.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Ask about the poster.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Ask what the home means before asking what the file says.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Do not make him perform misery. Let him explain consequences.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: His apartment is not just a location. It is the thing at stake.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Look at the window from inside and outside.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: A poster can be small. Eviction is not.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Bring back facts, not just pity.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Kowalski complained when a child learned recorder. Twice.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: If he did not complain about the poster, that is evidence disguised as silence.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: He watches the stairwell like it owes him testimony.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Ask him whether the poster disturbed him.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Do not ask about the lift unless you have packed lunch.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: His notebooks are terrifying but occasionally useful.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: He will describe the typography. Endure it.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Listen for the sentence where he admits no one cared.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: A man who complains about everything is valuable when he says nothing happened.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Bring patience. He eats time.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Zielinska knows what management pretends not to see.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: She notices patterns without making a festival of herself.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Ask her about other windows.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Ask her when the warnings began.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Ask whether Waldek was treated differently after asking why.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: She will not exaggerate for you. That is why she is useful.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: She has the calm face of someone who has already checked the facts.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: If there were other displays, she will know which ones survived.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Targeting is proved by pattern, not by a feeling in the corridor.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Go ask her before the landlord tidies the story.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
context: hint toward Landlord's Office / Administrator Beton
text: The landlord's office is where words go to become heavier.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
context: hint toward Landlord's Office / Administrator Beton
text: Administrator Beton speaks like a door with a tie.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
context: hint toward Landlord's Office / Administrator Beton
text: Ask for the building rules.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
context: hint toward Landlord's Office / Administrator Beton
text: Ask for the complaint record.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
context: hint toward Landlord's Office / Administrator Beton
text: If they say harmony, ask them to define it without humming.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
context: hint toward Landlord's Office / Administrator Beton
text: If they say residents complained, ask which residents and when.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
context: hint toward Landlord's Office / Administrator Beton
text: A rule that appears only after anger is not a rule. It is a costume.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
context: hint toward Landlord's Office / Administrator Beton
text: Do not let polished folders frighten you.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
context: hint toward Landlord's Office / Administrator Beton
text: Polish does not make a weak date stronger.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
context: hint toward Landlord's Office / Administrator Beton
text: Bring Crab if numbers start behaving strangely.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Waldek still has his home?
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Good.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: One window should not cost a person a kitchen table.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: The poster may be ugly. That is why walls are patient.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: I saw Mr. Pig walking like a man whose ship found soup.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Asia looked happy for four seconds before the phone rang.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Zielinska brought flowers. Kowalski brought corrections.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: The building will grumble, but it has learned a word: proportion.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Well done, young lawyer.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Homes are quieter when they are safe.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Have you seen the windows?
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Suddenly everyone owns tape.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: One poster becomes ten when people realise fear is optional.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: The landlord will call it disorder. I call it Tuesday with courage.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Do not worry. Half of them are crooked. Democracy often is.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Kowalski is measuring them all. This may become a second case.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Zielinska says pluralism has bad margins. I agree.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: If management writes a new rule, I hope they use smaller words and better reasons.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: The pigeons dislike the orange poster most. They have no legal basis.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Good. Let the building learn windows.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: That did not sound like a full victory.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Do not leave Waldek alone with the order.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Read the timetable.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Find what evidence was missing.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: A home case can still have another step if you move quickly.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Do not let shame slow you.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: The landlord will move faster than sympathy.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Asia will help you find the next deadline.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Zielinska may know what changed after court.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: And tell Waldek he is not a file, whatever the papers say.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
context: street reaction to printer sabotage beginning
text: The pigeons are nervous.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
context: street reaction to printer sabotage beginning
text: That means either rain or lawyers hiding something.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
context: street reaction to printer sabotage beginning
text: People say filings are disappearing.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
context: street reaction to printer sabotage beginning
text: Not lost. Disappearing.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
context: street reaction to printer sabotage beginning
text: There is a difference. Lost paper looks embarrassed. Disappeared paper looks useful to someone.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
context: street reaction to printer sabotage beginning
text: Your office printer made noises at dawn.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
context: street reaction to printer sabotage beginning
text: I am not saying it is guilty.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
context: street reaction to printer sabotage beginning
text: I am saying machines do not usually learn timing without help.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
context: street reaction to printer sabotage beginning
text: Look in the Business District.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
context: street reaction to printer sabotage beginning
text: Polished shoes leave fewer crumbs but better motives.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: A junior lawyer ran past holding blank pages and old fear.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: The court clerk looked at the filing counter like it had betrayed family.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Three firms had missing attachments today. One firm smiled too early.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: The print shop lights were on after closing.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: A courier cursed so loudly the pigeons left in formation.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: If filings vanish only near deadlines, that is not weather.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Paper does not choose enemies by itself.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: The Business District is very clean for a place that loses so much.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Ask the people who sell toner. They hear sins before priests do.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: If the printer is innocent, it deserves an apology and maintenance.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Find the young man who speaks to machines like they disappoint him personally.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Bajtek knows wires from rumours.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: If he says logs, bring him logs.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: If he says device, do not touch it with heroic fingers.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: He does not look cheerful. That is because he understands printers.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Tell him the old woman says machines do not lie, but people teach them tricks.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Technical evidence needs translation before judges trust it.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Bajtek can do that if no one interrupts him with superstition.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Bring coffee if you want him to speak in full sentences.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: And do not call the printer haunted.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: I saw that Szpon man near the print shop.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: His shoes made no sound. Bad sign.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: People who walk quietly near deadlines should be watched loudly.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: He smiled at a folder as if it owed him money.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: The pigeons moved away from him. Sensible birds.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Do not accuse him without paper.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Men like that eat accusations and ask for costs.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Find an invoice.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Find a device.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Then let his smile become evidence.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: The print shop has been busy at odd hours.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Paper trucks arrive before breakfast now.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: The owner sweeps the doorway too carefully.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Ask what was repaired.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Ask who paid.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Ask why a rival firm needs emergency toner near your deadline.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Receipts are gossip that learned arithmetic.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Do not be fooled by a clean counter.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: The dirt is usually in the invoice.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: If Crab comes with you, he will smell the dates.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: There goes the young lawyer with the filing.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: The pigeons have cleared the route. Mostly because of the scooter.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Do not hit the cabbage stand. It has already testified in another matter.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: If you drop page three, I will not chase it. I am old, not unwise.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: The court is north-east and the deadline is rude.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Faster, but keep the stamp page dry.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: That puddle has destroyed better arguments.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: A filing delivered by scooter is still a filing if the judge can read it.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: I have seen worse transport. Once a man filed by hat.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Go. Paper ages quickly near clocks.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: So the printer was framed.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: I knew it. It lacked motive.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Mr. Pig is preparing remarks for the machine, I hear.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Bajtek walked past looking less angry at physics.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Szpon's smile has become thinner. That is almost music.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: The court accepted the filing?
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Good.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: A door blocked by wires is still a blocked door.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Apologise to the printer if you must, but check the basement again.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: People who sabotage paper rarely stop at one page.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: You look like the court wanted cleaner logs.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: That happens when machines know more than lawyers can explain.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Go back to Bajtek.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Find the sequence.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Find the connection.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Find the prejudice.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Do not go in waving a device like a dead fish.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Technical truth needs a neat coat before court.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Szpon will not wait for your confidence to improve.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Try again while the paper is still warm.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
context: street reaction to Swine returning with arbitration trouble
text: Mr. Swine is back.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
context: street reaction to Swine returning with arbitration trouble
text: I know because three people asked where to buy expensive-looking excuses.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
context: street reaction to Swine returning with arbitration trouble
text: He brought luggage that sounded guilty on the cobblestones.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
context: street reaction to Swine returning with arbitration trouble
text: There is a bill attached, yes?
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
context: street reaction to Swine returning with arbitration trouble
text: Men who return from mountains with invoices usually signed something near dessert.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
context: street reaction to Swine returning with arbitration trouble
text: Do not laugh too early. Commercial trouble wears good shoes.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
context: street reaction to Swine returning with arbitration trouble
text: Find the paper he signed.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
context: street reaction to Swine returning with arbitration trouble
text: Find the words he misunderstood.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
context: street reaction to Swine returning with arbitration trouble
text: Find the amount that became ambitious.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
context: street reaction to Swine returning with arbitration trouble
text: And keep him away from ribbons.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Airport Road is open now. That is how you know the problem has become international.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Suitcases lie less than people but only because they have fewer pockets.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Mr. Swine's luggage had the posture of evidence.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: If there is a customs form, read the boring boxes.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Travel documents are where intention goes when people are too tired to lie beautifully.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: The airport announcements sound calmer than the passengers.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Do not let souvenirs distract you from dates.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: A signed thing from abroad is still a signed thing here.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Find the draft before the invoice writes history.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: And never sign after dessert. I have said this for years.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
context: hint toward Ms. Tanaka / translation issue
text: The translation officer will not save you with magic.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
context: hint toward Ms. Tanaka / translation issue
text: She may save you with precision, which is better.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
context: hint toward Ms. Tanaka / translation issue
text: Ask what the words could mean.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
context: hint toward Ms. Tanaka / translation issue
text: Ask what they could not safely mean.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
context: hint toward Ms. Tanaka / translation issue
text: Ask whether politeness became a bill by accident or by design.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
context: hint toward Ms. Tanaka / translation issue
text: Do not make the foreign words the joke.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
context: hint toward Ms. Tanaka / translation issue
text: The joke is Mr. Swine treating ceremony like weather.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
context: hint toward Ms. Tanaka / translation issue
text: Bring the original and the draft.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
context: hint toward Ms. Tanaka / translation issue
text: A translation without context is a pigeon without a square.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
context: hint toward Ms. Tanaka / translation issue
text: It flies, but no one knows why.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: The arbitration will want clean issues.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Contract first.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Authority second.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Amount third.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Remedy last.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Do not let Mr. Swine speak on all four unless you enjoy financial theatre.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Let Dr. A. Cula argue law.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Let Mr. Swine say what happened, if memory permits.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Let the documents carry the heavy things.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: And if pudding appears in the invoice, do not laugh until after you count it.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: So the mountain did not eat the firm.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Good.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Mr. Swine looked almost honest when he came back. Frightening development.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Ms. Tanaka walked past with the calm of someone who fixed a sentence.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Crab was muttering about line items. That means victory in his language.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Mr. Pig said the ship survived foreign waters. I reminded him we are inland.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: A smaller bill is not innocence, but it is breathable.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Tell Mr. Swine to stop signing things with ribbons.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Tell Asia to lock the travel drawer.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: And tell the printer I said nothing. It is listening again.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Commercial trouble is harder when charm already signed the receipt.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: If the award is bad, read what the tribunal accepted.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Was it contract?
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Authority?
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Amount?
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Remedy?
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Find the weak plank before stepping again.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Ms. Tanaka's note may still help if used carefully.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Do not let Mr. Swine explain law to anyone.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: And hide the ribbons.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
context: street reaction when mass closure notices arrive
text: The notices arrived this morning.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
context: street reaction when mass closure notices arrive
text: Not one notice. Many.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
context: street reaction when mass closure notices arrive
text: That is how cities speak when they do not want to hear replies.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
context: street reaction when mass closure notices arrive
text: People stood in the square reading the same words and fearing different things.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
context: street reaction when mass closure notices arrive
text: That is not clarity.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
context: street reaction when mass closure notices arrive
text: The bakery got one.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
context: street reaction when mass closure notices arrive
text: The clinic got one.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
context: street reaction when mass closure notices arrive
text: Pig & Swine got one.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
context: street reaction when mass closure notices arrive
text: Even the bench has begun looking temporary.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
context: street reaction when mass closure notices arrive
text: Young lawyer, this is bigger than your office now.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Welcome to Protest Square.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: It was called Fountain Square before the fountain became an access issue.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: People came because the notices frightened them differently.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Some fear losing homes.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Some fear losing work.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Some fear losing the only door that did not ask for a password.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Ask them what the notice did to them.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Do not just ask whether they dislike the plan.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Dislike is small. Consequence is evidence.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Take statements. Then take them seriously.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Talk to the quiet ones first.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: The loud ones will still be loud later.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Ask what service they use.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Ask what the notice told them.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Ask what they did next.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Ask what they could not do.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: If someone says they did not understand, write that plainly.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: If someone says they missed the objection route, ask why.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: If someone depended on Pig & Swine, ask how.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Impact is not a chant. It is a sentence with a consequence.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: City Hall has the plan.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Plans are where harm wears clean margins.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Ask for the notice templates.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Ask for individual assessments.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Ask who handled objections.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: If they say everyone had a chance, ask what happened to the chances.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: City Hall loves queues because queues make delay look democratic.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Bring patience, a pen, and someone who distrusts invoices.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Crab, perhaps.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: The pigeons avoid City Hall. Too many windows, not enough crumbs.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: If everyone received the same notice, someone decided difference was inconvenient.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Find the template.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Then find the people it failed to describe.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: A shop is not a home.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: A law office is not a bakery.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: A clinic is not a vacant corridor.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Same paper, different wounds.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: That is the argument.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Do not say copy-paste like a joke. Say absence of assessment.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: The Court will understand that better.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
context: hint about objection system / industrial printer
text: People say they objected and heard nothing.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
context: hint about objection system / industrial printer
text: Nothing is sometimes a decision wearing soft shoes.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
context: hint about objection system / industrial printer
text: Find where the objections went.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
context: hint about objection system / industrial printer
text: If a machine sorted them, ask who taught it categories.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
context: hint about objection system / industrial printer
text: Duplicate.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
context: hint about objection system / industrial printer
text: Late.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
context: hint about objection system / industrial printer
text: Not helpful.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
context: hint about objection system / industrial printer
text: Those words can close doors if no one watches them.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
context: hint about objection system / industrial printer
text: Bring Bajtek.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
context: hint about objection system / industrial printer
text: And do not let anyone call it just a printer.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Szpon is involved again?
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Of course he is.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Men like that do not disappear. They become frameworks.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: He will say modernisation.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Ask him who was heard.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: He will say efficiency.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Ask him who was lost.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: He will say public interest.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Ask him which public objected and where their objections went.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Then watch his shoes.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
context: hint about Swine social utility ledger
text: Mr. Swine has been hiding kindness badly.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
context: hint about Swine social utility ledger
text: That is better than hiding cruelty well, but still a problem.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
context: hint about Swine social utility ledger
text: If he kept a ledger, make him show it.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
context: hint about Swine social utility ledger
text: Not because he is a saint.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
context: hint about Swine social utility ledger
text: Because the Court needs facts.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
context: hint about Swine social utility ledger
text: Who came through the door?
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
context: hint about Swine social utility ledger
text: Who got help?
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
context: hint about Swine social utility ledger
text: Who would be turned away by the new system?
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
context: hint about Swine social utility ledger
text: Pig & Swine is ridiculous, yes.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
context: hint about Swine social utility ledger
text: But sometimes ridiculous places let people in.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Put the right person with the right point.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Kowalski for confusion.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Zielinska for community consequence.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Bajtek for the machine.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Crab for the money trail.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Grzyb for reluctant formalism.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Swine for the ledger, if he can be honest for ten minutes.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Whimsy for the rights language, but leash the thunder.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Mr. Murrow for structure.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Asia for the file, because otherwise all of you become furniture.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: The Supreme Court is north beyond the square.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: The stairs look dramatic because architects also enjoy pressure.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Bring the Final Brief.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Bring the people.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Bring the logs.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Bring the ledger.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Bring the notices that failed people before they knew they had failed.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Do not bring only anger.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Anger opens mouths. Evidence opens doors.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Go, young lawyer. Make the city answer in full sentences.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: You are going in?
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Good.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Remember what the case is.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Not just Pig & Swine.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Not just an office with a broken printer and too many crises.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: People came there because polished doors were closed.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: If the city wants a new system, let it build one that opens.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: If it cannot, let the old ridiculous door stand until it can.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Tell the Court that.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: But with exhibits, please.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: So the scheme is stopped for now.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Correct notices. Restored objections. Real assessment.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Good.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: The city has been asked to speak like people live here.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: That is not too much to ask.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Pig & Swine remains open?
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Then the door stays noisy.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Noisy is better than locked.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: The pigeons approve. They have surrounded the bench in committee formation.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Well done, young lawyer.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: A partial victory is still a door held open.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: The city must correct the process?
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Good.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: People get to object again?
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Better.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Pig & Swine has time?
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Use it.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Do not become respectable too quickly. It may confuse your clients.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Asia will know where to put the judgment.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: The pigeons request a copy for review.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Temporary protection, then?
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: That is not nothing.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: A little time can be a bridge if people use it before it rots.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Read the order carefully.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Find every deadline.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Tell the people in the square what changed and what did not.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Do not let hope become confusion.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: The city will move again.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: So must you.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: I will keep the bench warm.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: The Court did not stop it?
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Then grief later. Timetable now.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Find who still has individual remedies.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Find who missed the objection route.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Find whether new evidence can still be used.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Do not let one judgment turn people into boxes.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: The city will say the matter is finished.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Finished for whom?
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Ask that.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: And do not leave Pig & Swine alone tonight.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: The office sign is still crooked.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Good. Straight signs become arrogant.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: People still come through the door.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Asia still knows where everything is.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Mr. Pig still talks about ships on dry land.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Mr. Murrow still looks like the ending needed footnotes.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Crab bought new folders and denies it was emotional.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: Whimsy has written a slogan too long for any wall.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: The printer is quiet today.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: I do not trust it, but I respect its growth.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: The Old Woman kept feeding the pigeons and correcting young lawyers before they reached court.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: The Old Woman became an unofficial landmark: turn left at the bench where procedure is judged by crumbs.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: The Old Woman never joined Pig & Swine, but several clients found the office because she pointed at it with bread.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: The Old Woman still distrusts identical notices and polished shoes.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: The Old Woman named one pigeon Proportionality. It bites only when necessary.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: The Old Woman keeps a copy of the Supreme Court judgment under the biscuit tin.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: The Old Woman says Pig & Swine survived because ridiculous doors are still doors.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: The Old Woman once told Szpon to stop frightening the pigeons. He has avoided the bench since.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: The Old Woman remains the Legal Quarter's memory, weather station, and informal appellate conscience.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Old Woman
text: The Old Woman's final advice: keep the stamped copy, feed the birds, and never trust a notice that cannot explain itself.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.
